title: Capstone C: Hydrogen Value-Chain Integration
caption: Parameter study for Capstone C: Hydrogen Value-Chain Integration.
description: The chapter parameter study converts an end-to-end H2 value-chain screening case from feed to end-user into a small sweep with visible KPIs: delivered cost of hydrogen at the end-user, carbon intensity from well-to-product, end-to-end energy efficiency, and integrated risk register. The values are screening outputs intended to demonstrate the notebook structure and discussion pattern.
discussion: The parameter study shows how an end-to-end H2 value-chain screening case from feed to end-user responds when the controlling parameter changes. The important result is not a single base-case value; it is the shape of the response and the point where delivered cost of hydrogen at the end-user, carbon intensity from well-to-product, end-to-end energy efficiency, and integrated risk register begin to trade against margin or cost.
calculation_basis: Chapter-specific screening calculations for an end-to-end H2 value-chain screening case from feed to end-user, normalized to the KPIs: delivered cost of hydrogen at the end-user, carbon intensity from well-to-product, end-to-end energy efficiency, and integrated risk register.
study_basis: IEA hydrogen route context and the cited process-design references define the interpretation envelope for the normalized indices.
source_labels: IEA Global Hydrogen Review 2023, Chemical engineering design reference
